steps.title: Test the student's certainty
steps.content: The student claims IGF-1 gene variations must account for body-size variance among dog breeds. To undermine that claim, a scientist must show that IGF-1 variation is not the only relevant cause.

steps.title: Reject supportive quotes
steps.content: B does not weaken the idea that IGF-1 variants matter. C directly supports the connection between IGF-1 variation and body size. D shifts to whether conclusions apply outside dogs, not whether the student's dog-size claim is too strong.

steps.title: Undercut the sole cause
steps.content: The quote saying the IGF-1-related gene is one of multiple size-related genes weakens the claim that those variations must account for the observed variance. The answer is A.
